goal: Implement executable pre-implementation gates so work cannot be called ready without expected outcome, risk, evidence, and stop criteria.
risk: AOF starts implementation and later invents success criteria, making audits technically green but human expectations opaque.
loss_boundary: No implementation-ready claim is acceptable without goal, risk, loss boundary, acceptance gates, evidence plan, maker/checker separation, and stop conditions.
acceptance_gates: work-readiness-record writes a schema-valid readiness artifact
work-readiness-audit fails missing or weak readiness records
release-state-audit includes work-readiness-audit for v6.9 and later
evidence_plan: schemas, runtime commands, CLI help, command registry, focused tests, release-state audit, Council review
stop_conditions: Stop if readiness audit cannot fail a missing gate
Stop if release-state audit cannot consume the readiness gate
